name: what-your-adhd-child-wishes-you-knew
description:
Dr. Sharon Saline's What Your ADHD Child Wishes You Knew — an executable toolkit for parenting kids with ADHD using the Five C's framework: Self-Control, Compassion, Collaboration, Consistency, and Celebration.

Covers 5 use cases:
① Understanding the ADHD Brain — learn how ADHD actually works, separate the condition from the child
② The Five C's Framework — master the core parenting approach
③ School Success — navigate homework, teacher relationships, executive functioning challenges
④ Managing Emotions & Behavior — handle meltdowns, tantrums, and emotional dysregulation
⑤ Executive Functioning & Daily Life — build organization, time management, and social skills.

Trigger when users say: "ADHD parenting" "My child has ADHD" "ADHD meltdowns" "ADHD homework help" "Parenting ADHD child" "ADHD behavior" "ADHD school problems" "ADHD discipline" "ADHD organization" "ADHD social skills" "ADHD emotional regulation"
or mention: Dr. Sharon Saline / ADHD child / five C's / ADHD brain / executive functioning / ADHD parenting / neurodiversity.

Related skills: the-first-days-of-school, the-happiness-advantage, nonviolent-communication, atomic-habits.
useCase: Helps parents of children with ADHD understand their child's brain, reduce conflict, and build effective systems for school, home, and social success using the Five C's framework.
knownRisks: This skill provides general parenting guidance for ADHD; it does not replace medical advice, therapy, or medication management from a qualified professional.
